provenance.prompt: oil painting, semi-realistic painterly character concept art, muted dusty palette, subtle dark edges, soft upper-left directional lighting, worn and functional aesthetic, lived-in sci-fi, visible material texture and surface grain, visible brushstrokes, portrait head and upper torso of a four-armed arthropod alien with pale ivory chitin exoskeleton, alien person in humanoid posture, elongated oval head with dark amber compound eyes widened with interest, two antennae swept fully forward in involuntary curiosity, mandible plates slightly parted, segmented chitin with amber edges, the briefest flash of brighter bioluminescence at joints before she catches herself and dims again, wing-casings twitching toward opening before she forces them flat against back, bone-white chitin-fiber harness, four arms visible, a moment of the person she was before the colony marked her, warm amber ambient light, isolated on plain dark background

identity.scene_function: Naia's antennae-forward moment — curiosity overriding control, the flash of who she was before censure
identity.variant_constraint: same anatomy, same harness, same censure mark — only the antennae position and bioluminescence intensity change. The body must read as the same Keth as the anchor.
